CHAPTER V
THE EMERGING PRINCIPLES
OF INTERPRETATION
Let It be stated at the outset that the Vlmshottari dosha is
misused mostly and rarely used properly. The best way of removing
this ddkency Is to use simultaneously another dosha, as a support,
as a supplementary aid, or, as a confirmatory check for the prediction
odng given.
All old and experienced astrologers use various dosha to narrow
down the area of confusion like the Yoglnl dasha, the Ashtottari
dasha, some of the specific cfosihas given in Brlhat Parashara Hora
Shastra, which the translators or commentators have merely
reproduced without themselves knowing how to use them. They
have nelghther learned these different dashas nor have they done
their own researches to remove this deficiency.
The problem then is how to use the Vlmshortarl dosha with less
confusion, it should be done by noting down ihe principles that
emerge out of It, for which the dasha of each planet must be used
In full three-fold manner; the mahadasha (major period); antnrdasha
(sub-period): and the praiyantar dosha (sub-sub period}. Maharshl
Parashara prescribes the use of five-Fold division by including
sookshma dasha (5ul>sub-sub period) and also prana dasha (sub-sub-
sub-sub-perlod).
A result-yielding and effective method still Is to use -
(a) The mahadasha (major period)
(b) The antardasha (sub period)*
(c) The pratyantar dastia (sub-sub period) and
(d) Transits of planets duimg the duration of the pratyantar dasha
(sub-sub periods).
Yet to use the Vlmshottari dasha In this manner some vital
.pdndpi.es must be kept In mind.

NATURAL SIGNIFICANCE OF THE

Not as an excuse but as necessity^ 1 have to discuss first, to

and that of two Oriya disciples of a ftaba.
Begum Akhtar
The late Begum Akhtof, as immortal GhazAI sfngci*, figures
prominently in my stories of wishes fulfilled. As boys In sub teens
playing football In a ground adjacent to a three sloncyed ycJJow
building we used to hear the melodious voice of the great Akhtaft
Bah Boys of the Queen's Anglo-Sanslirlt School. Lucknow (now a
■college) knew about the legendary Akhtari Bai and her great mchfiis?
and some of us naturally had wondered and wished If we could ever
attend one—the forbidden zone, socially tabooed by orthodox
society, particularly orthodox Hindu society.
Her sky rocketing fame and the number of the male admirers

In all the writings of writers on astrological subjects, the relation
between astrology and karma has been discussed sometime or the
other. &ui no writer has posctf the most Impoitanc question whether
the two great Authorities we follow, Mnlujrshl Parashara and

further question, to which the writers should have addressed
themselves Is whether astrology gets directly related to the belief In
the past* the present birth and the future births,
Brihat Parashara HoraShastm starts with refcrenoeto the Avtara
Krama supporting the Hindu belief that God reincarnates himself in
this world whenever the halanee of evil begins to outweigh heavily
the virtues, In such situation nine avfaras take birth each under the
beneficent Influence of a particular ptancL Lord Rama from the Sun*
Lord Krishna from the Moon, Lord NarAslmh.i from the Mars, lord
Budha from Mercury, Lord Varnan from juplter. Lord Pajshuram from
Venus, Lord Kuftm from Saturn, Lord Sooker from ftahu and Lord
Meena from Ketu. It should be icmembered that Brihat Parashara
Hora Shastra starts with the story of divine reincarnation*
Where and how does Parashara refer to human reincarnation?
Such references are scattered In many places in Pamshara's texts,
some of which can be seen first. The eighth house Is referred to asr
Purvapara fanuF which means the occupation, the profession
of the past birth. The next important reference Is when Parashara
deals with the maraka or the periods of death. A child may find It
difficult to survive In rhe first four years because of the evil deeds of
the mother,, (n the next four years as a result of bad fctmrasofthe
father and In the last four years upto the 12* year due to child's own
bad karmas. The nature of death Is also decided of every Individual
as a result of the karma of the past life, it can be as a result of royal
punishment, incurable diseases* through weapons, poisoning,
